domain_hint: Titanium-PEEK lumbar interbody fusion cage structural FEA, quasi-static compressive loading, ASTM F2077, 510(k) regulatory submission
ambiguity_notes: (1) Element formulation contradiction: mesh convergence study used C3D8R hex elements but production model uses C3D10 tet elements, and the model team asserts the GCI transfers without numerical support. (2) Peak stress vs. fatigue limit contradiction: the executive summary claims stresses are within acceptable limits while the detailed results show 718 MPa exceeding the 550 MPa fatigue limit, with the localization argument not formally documented. (3) Stiffness vs. strain discrepancy: FEA overpredicts subsidence stiffness (+7.3%) but underpredicts surface strain (-5.7%), with no reconciliation analysis performed, creating contradictory validation signals. (4) Element distortion contradiction: slide narrative states Jacobian ratio 0.31 is borderline acceptable, but the model log states several elements exceed distortion limits, and the threshold interpretation cited in the slide is internally inconsistent.
